framework: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers (AI-CFT) 2024
level_assignment_policy: AI-CFT levels are cumulative and assigned only here, at the portfolio level, by aggregating per-worksheet LO evidence. Per-worksheet Gate 4 emits LO evidence (confirmed / weak / none); it never assigns a level. The level below is provisional. The researcher makes the final holistic judgement; the system does not finalize it automatically.

student_profile.note: Pre-service primary mathematics teacher, year 3, no programming background. This profile sets a realistic expected ceiling at Acquire, making Deepen evidence particularly noteworthy.

extraction_failures.description: Numeric table not extracted. Conceptual text captured instead.
extraction_failures.impact: Gate 3/4 blocked for WS10 table items

extraction_failures.description: 8 of 15 items appear to be extracted from wrong sections (A-D, E Q1). Vocabulary definitions extracted instead of CODAP inquiry answers.
extraction_failures.impact: Core Deepen items (A-D sections) cannot be scored

extraction_failures.description: Q10 multi-select, Q11 ordering, Q12 AI identification not captured.
extraction_failures.impact: 3 cognitive items missing from final WS

extraction_failures.description: Part 1 path matching (A/B/C to given rules) not extracted.

data_quality_flags.reliability_note: Portfolio synthesis draws on 6 worksheets with reliable data (WS1, WS3, WS4, WS5, WS6, WS7 Part 2) plus partial data from WS_DT (F_Q2, G_Q2, E metrics) and WS11 (B8a/b, B9). WS10 table and WS_DT A-D sections require re-extraction before the portfolio can be considered complete.

WS1.key_evidence: 9 full, 2 partial. All core DT vocabulary correct. Sensitivity/MCR formulas correct. Overfitting partial (one-sided).

WS3.key_evidence: 2 of 3 classifications correct (B1 wrong). Variable mismatch in B7/B8 (sugar used instead of energy). Reason blanks not extracted.

WS4.key_evidence: 5 of 5 text items full credit. MCR formula correct with arithmetic. Metric-based criterion. Correct peer claim evaluation.

WS5.key_evidence: 4 rows fully correct, 1 partial (row 5 MCR=0.25 error). Strong tie-breaking justification.

WS6.key_evidence: Valid 2-level tree. Root=şeker, second=yağ. All leaves labeled. Correct operators. B12 (leaf count) blank, minor.

WS7.key_evidence: Part 2 (own rules): 3 of 3 full credit, consistent with WS6. Part 1 (path matching): not captured. Band partial due to data gap, not wrong answers.

WS10.key_evidence: Extraction failure on the numeric table. Conceptual prose shows strong understanding: midpoint logic, n-1 candidates, O(n log n). Application unverified.

WS11.key_evidence: B8a/b: correct rule application to new example (Çikolata). B9: complete DT definition. Q10/Q11/Q12: not captured.

WS_DT.key_evidence: F_Q2: overfitting correctly explained (Create signal). E metrics consistent (0.75/0.20). A-D sections: wrong-section extraction, not scored. score_ratio understated.

pattern_1_concept_application_gap.name: Vocabulary ahead of application
pattern_1_concept_application_gap.description: Student defines concepts correctly in WS1 but shows gaps when applying them to real data in WS3.

evidence.WS1_B4_threshold_definition: full — 'bir özelliği iki gruba ayıran sınır sayısı'
evidence.WS3_B7_B8_threshold_application: zero — used wrong variable (şeker instead of enerji for Leo's rule)
evidence.WS_DT_E_Q1_metric_preference: zero — extracted as definition, not preference (extraction issue may mask true answer)

pattern_1_concept_application_gap.interpretation: The gap between WS1 (define threshold correctly) and WS3 (apply the correct threshold variable) is the clearest concept-application gap. Student knows what a threshold IS but confused which variable Leo's rule uses. This may reflect reading comprehension issue (not checking which variable was specified) rather than a conceptual gap.
pattern_1_concept_application_gap.resolution_note: WS4 MCR formula applied correctly with own chosen variable. Suggests the WS3 error was variable-specific (enerji vs. şeker) not formula-level.

pattern_2_progression_curve.name: WS3 → WS4 → WS5 improvement
pattern_2_progression_curve.description: Student shows clear progression from threshold application (WS3, partial) to best threshold selection (WS4, full) to systematic exploration (WS5, full).

evidence.WS3: partial — 2/3 classifications correct, variable mismatch in own threshold
evidence.WS4: full — best threshold selected with MCR criterion and correct arithmetic

pattern_2_progression_curve.interpretation: The three WS form a clear progression: apply a given rule → choose the best rule → systematically find the best rule. Student performance improves at each step. This is pedagogically coherent: the ProDaBi sequence is working as designed.
pattern_2_progression_curve.aicft_significance: WS3 = Acquire (apply given rule). WS4 = Deepen (choose using criterion). WS5 = Deepen (systematic search + tie-breaking). Progression confirms consolidation of Deepen competency across the unplugged sequence.

pattern_3_unplugged_plugged_consistency.name: WS7 rules vs. WS_DT C/D sections
pattern_3_unplugged_plugged_consistency.description: Check whether student's unplugged rule-writing (WS7) is consistent with their digital tree exploration (WS_DT).

evidence.WS7_rules: şeker ≤ 10 AND yağ ≤ 5 → recommended; şeker ≤ 10 AND yağ > 5 → not; şeker > 10 → not
evidence.WS_DT_B_Q4: Şeker miktarı 10 gramdan az... (extraction likely wrong section)

pattern_3_unplugged_plugged_consistency.interpretation: WS7 rules are internally consistent with WS6. WS_DT A-D extraction failures prevent full plugged-unplugged consistency check. However, the WS_DT content referencing şeker as a variable (even if misextracted) suggests the same feature persisted into the digital activity.
pattern_3_unplugged_plugged_consistency.confidence: low — re-extraction needed for WS_DT A-D

pattern_4_overfitting_trajectory.name: Partial → full overfitting understanding
pattern_4_overfitting_trajectory.description: Student's overfitting understanding deepens across the sequence.

evidence.WS1_B10: partial — 'eğitim verisine çok uyum sağlayıp genelleyememesi' — one-sided definition (mentions poor generalization but not the training-test performance contrast explicitly)
evidence.WS_DT_F_Q2: full — 'aşırı öğrenirse eğitim verisinde iyi, test verisinde kötü sonuç verir' — explicit two-sided: train-good, test-poor

pattern_4_overfitting_trajectory.interpretation: WS1 definition was one-sided. After hands-on CODAP train/test evaluation (Section F), student articulates the two-sided contrast clearly. The digital activity deepened the concept beyond what the unplugged vocabulary exercise captured. This is exactly the kind of learning trajectory the sequence is designed to produce.
pattern_4_overfitting_trajectory.aicft_significance: WS1 → WS_DT F_Q2 trajectory is evidence of LO3.2.3 (Deepen) and approaching LO3.3.1 (Create). Student moved from one-sided definition to two-sided causal understanding with specific conditions.

pattern_5_metacognitive_self_awareness.name: Accurate self-assessment in WS11 reflections
pattern_5_metacognitive_self_awareness.description: WS11 reflections corroborate scoring pattern from other worksheets.

evidence.WS11_B3_self_report: Duyarlılık ve MCR formüllerini başta karıştırdım, sonunda netleşti.
evidence.WS1_B6_actual_score: partial — implicit not explicit TP definition
evidence.WS11_B2_hardest_ws: WS5 en çok zaman aldı ama en çok şeyi de orada öğrendim.
evidence.WS5_actual_score: full — strongest Deepen evidence in unplugged sequence
evidence.WS11_B4_tree_difficulty: Ağaç çizmek düşündüğümden zordu; dalları doğru yönde etiketlemek dikkat istedi.
evidence.WS6_B12_actual: blank — only missing item in WS6

pattern_5_metacognitive_self_awareness.interpretation: Student's self-reports are accurate: formula confusion (WS1 B6 partial) resolved by end; WS5 was hard but productive (confirmed by full credit); tree branch labeling was difficult (confirmed by WS6 B12 blank). This metacognitive accuracy is itself a positive sign — students who can accurately identify their struggles and learning are developing the reflective capacity relevant to AI-CFT Create level.
pattern_5_metacognitive_self_awareness.aicft_significance: LO3.3.1 signal — accurate metacognition about AI learning process

Acquire.required_minimum: WS1 core vocabulary + WS3 threshold application + WS11 B8a (apply rule to new example)
evidence: WS1: All 11 vocabulary items correct (9 full, 2 partial) — LO3.1.1, LO3.1.2
WS3: 2/3 classifications correct (B3, B5 full) — LO3.1.1 partial
WS7 Part 2: 3 correct if-then rules — LO3.1.1
WS11 B8a: Correctly applies own rule to Çikolata (new example) — LO3.1.1
WS11 B9: Complete DT definition — LO3.1.2
gaps: WS3 B1: Popcorn misclassified (applied wrong variable)
WS7 Part 1: Path matching not captured — given-tree reading unverified

Deepen.required_minimum: Metric-based threshold comparison + systematic threshold search + two-sided overfitting understanding
evidence: WS4 B2: Threshold selection criterion is explicitly MCR-based — LO3.2.1
WS4 B3: Two-threshold comparison with specific MCR values — LO3.2.1
WS4 B5: Correct evaluation of peer claim with reasoning — LO3.2.2
WS5 B25: Tie-breaking among equal-MCR thresholds (parsimony reasoning) — LO3.2.1, LO3.2.2
WS_DT F_Q2: Train/test performance gap explained — LO3.2.3
WS10 B5-B7: Finite threshold candidate set correctly explained — LO3.2.1
gaps: WS_DT A-D: Data-driven variable selection and model comparison not verifiable (extraction failure)
WS_DT E_Q1: Metric preference justification not captured (wrong extraction)

Create.required_minimum: Explanation of why perfect classification is impossible + overfitting trade-off articulation + metacognitive reflection on AI learning limitations
evidence_present: WS_DT F_Q2: Overfitting articulated as train/test gap — strong Create signal — LO3.3.1
WS10 B8: O(n log n) complexity analysis — beyond expected level — LO3.3.1 signal
WS11 B3: Accurate self-diagnosis of prior confusion resolved — metacognitive awareness — LO3.3.1 signal
evidence_missing: WS_DT E_Q4: Did not answer 'No — DT cannot achieve perfect classification' with data overlap explanation — needed for LO3.3.1
WS_DT G_Q1: Described algorithm process, not what model 'learned' — needed for LO3.3.1
WS_DT A-D: Data-driven model design and evaluation — extraction failure
Create.create_conclusion: Create evidence is present but insufficient for confirmation. F_Q2 is the strongest signal. E_Q4 and G_Q1 are the two items that would tip the assessment to confirmed Create — but E_Q4 did not address the question, and G_Q1 was process-focused. Pending re-extraction of WS_DT A-D sections.

final_aicft_assessment.confidence_note: Acquire and Deepen are confirmed across multiple worksheets with reliable extraction. Create is approaching but not confirmed.
final_aicft_assessment.justification: Student consistently demonstrates metric-based reasoning (WS4, WS5), systematic exploration (WS5), valid tree construction (WS6), correct rule formulation (WS7), and hands-on application (WS11 B8a). Overfitting explanation in WS_DT F_Q2 is the single strongest Create signal. However, Create requires converging evidence from E_Q4 (impossibility of perfect classification), G_Q1 (model learning articulation), and full A-D data-driven reasoning — none of these are currently verified.
final_aicft_assessment.pedagogical_note: For a year-3 pre-service primary math teacher with no programming background, confirmed Deepen is a strong outcome. The ProDaBi sequence appears effective for this student — the unplugged-digital progression produced genuine conceptual deepening (WS1 overfitting partial → WS_DT F_Q2 full). The variable confusion in WS3 (enerji vs. şeker) is worth discussing in feedback: student applied her own variable instead of the given rule, which may reflect active sensemaking rather than rule-following error.

priority_reextraction_list.why: Core Deepen items unscored due to wrong-section extraction. These determine whether Create level is confirmed.
Student's algorithmic understanding is high from conceptual items but numeric table (the main task) not extracted.
Three cognitive items completely missing. These provide direct evidence of LO3.1.1 and LO3.1.5.
Reading a given tree (vs. writing own) is a separate competency. Path matching boxes A/B/C not captured.

strengths: MCR calculation and interpretation is solid — applied formula correctly in WS4 with step-by-step working.
Systematic exploration in WS5 is exemplary — completed 5 threshold trials, identified the tie correctly, applied a principled tie-breaking rule.
Tree construction in WS6 is structurally valid — correct operators, two different features, labeled leaves.
Rule formulation in WS7 (Part 2) is consistent with the tree — shows ability to translate structure into language.
Overfitting explanation (WS_DT F_Q2) is clear and complete — you identified both sides of the train/test gap correctly.
areas_for_growth: In WS3, the threshold was applied using the wrong variable (sugar instead of the required energy). When a worksheet specifies which variable to use, check before applying the rule.
Sensitivity definition (WS1 B6) mentioned 'correctly classified positives' but did not explicitly contrast with 'actual positives' — the two-sided condition is important for precision.

In WS_DT E Q4, the question asked whether a DT can achieve perfect classification. The answer should be No, with an explanation: some datasets have overlapping classes that no threshold can separate perfectly. This distinction is important for understanding model limitations.

In WS_DT G Q1, 'what did the model learn?' is asking about the output (the rules/patterns), not the process. The model learned: 'if sugar ≤ 10 AND fat ≤ 5 → recommended'. The how is the algorithm; the what is the learned rule.
